title: Messaging Apps
description: Send text messages, photos, and voice notes to family and friends using WhatsApp and more.

lessons.title: What are Messaging Apps?
lessons.content:
Messaging apps let you send instant written messages, photos, voice notes, and videos to anyone in the world — usually for free using Wi-Fi.

📱 Popular Messaging Apps:
• WhatsApp — the most popular in the UK. Free. Works on any phone. Perfect for family groups.
• iMessage — built into iPhones. Free between Apple devices.
• Facebook Messenger — chat with Facebook friends.
• Telegram — very similar to WhatsApp, with some extra features.
• Signal — focused on privacy and security.

For most seniors in the UK, WhatsApp is the most useful because nearly everyone — of all ages — already uses it.

✅ What can you do with WhatsApp?
• Send text messages — like texts but free
• Make voice calls (like a phone call, free over Wi-Fi)
• Make video calls (see each other's faces, free over Wi-Fi)
• Send photos, videos, and voice notes
• Create group chats with family or friends
• Share your location with family if you need help
• Send documents and PDFs

💡 vs Normal Text (SMS):
Normal texts cost money (part of your phone plan). WhatsApp messages use the internet (free on Wi-Fi) — a huge advantage especially for international contacts.
tips: WhatsApp is free on Wi-Fi — you can message and call family abroad at no extra cost.
Voice notes are great if typing is difficult — hold the microphone button, speak, and release to send.
WhatsApp is encrypted end-to-end, meaning only you and the recipient can read your messages.

lessons.title: Setting Up WhatsApp
lessons.content:
WhatsApp is free to download and easy to set up. You will need a smartphone with a phone number.

⚙️ Setting Up WhatsApp:
1. Open the App Store (iPhone) or Play Store (Android)
2. Search for 'WhatsApp' — look for the official green app with a phone icon
3. Tap 'Install' or 'Get' — it's free
4. Open WhatsApp and tap 'Agree and Continue'
5. Enter your mobile phone number including country code (+44 for UK, drop the first 0 of your number)
6. WhatsApp sends a 6-digit verification code to your phone by text — enter it
7. Enter your name and optionally add a profile photo
8. WhatsApp automatically finds which of your phone contacts also use WhatsApp

👥 WhatsApp will show your contacts who use it:
You do not need to 'add' them separately — they appear automatically if you have their number saved.

📸 Setting a Profile Photo:
• Tap the three dots (⋮) in the top right > Settings > tap your profile photo area
• Choose 'Take Photo' or 'Upload from Gallery'
• This helps family and friends recognise you

🔔 Notifications:
WhatsApp will ask to send you notifications — allow this so you see new messages arrive.
tips: Your profile photo is only visible to your contacts — not to strangers.
If you don't get the verification code by text within a minute, tap 'Call Me' to receive it by phone call.
Once set up, WhatsApp automatically stays logged in — you don't need to sign in each time.

lessons.title: Sending Messages, Photos, and Voice Notes
lessons.content:
Once WhatsApp is set up, messaging is simple.

💬 Sending a Text Message:
1. Open WhatsApp and tap on a contact's name
2. Tap in the message box at the bottom
3. Type your message using the keyboard
4. Tap the blue send arrow ➤ to send

You will see:
• One grey tick ✓ — message sent
• Two grey ticks ✓✓ — message delivered to their phone
• Two blue ticks ✓✓ — message has been read

📸 Sending a Photo:
1. In a chat, tap the paperclip 📎 or the camera icon 📷
2. Choose 'Gallery' to pick an existing photo, or 'Camera' to take a new one
3. Add a caption if you like, then tap send

🎙️ Sending a Voice Note:
Perfect if typing is difficult!
1. In a chat, hold your finger on the microphone icon 🎤 (bottom right)
2. Speak your message
3. Release your finger to send automatically
4. Or slide to the left to cancel without sending

😊 Using Emoji:
Tap the smiley face icon on the keyboard to add emoji (small pictures like 😊❤️👍). They add warmth and personality to messages.
tips: Voice notes are a wonderful alternative to typing — many people prefer them for longer messages.
You can see a preview of a photo before sending — check it's the right one before tapping send.
Double tap on any message you received to react to it with an emoji — a quick way to say 'I saw this!'

lessons.title: WhatsApp Group Chats
lessons.content:
Group chats let multiple people chat together in one place — perfect for family coordination.

👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Creating a Group Chat:
1. Open WhatsApp
2. Tap the three dots (⋮) > 'New Group' (Android) OR tap the chat icon then 'New Group' (iPhone)
3. Tap on the contacts you want to include
4. Tap the green arrow ➤
5. Give the group a name (e.g. 'The Smith Family')
6. Optionally add a group photo
7. Tap the green tick ✓ to create the group

💬 Using a Group Chat:
• Messages you send appear for everyone in the group
• Everyone's replies appear in the same chat thread
• You can see who said what (their name appears above their message)

📢 Group Admins:
When you create a group, you become the admin. You can:
• Add or remove members
• Change the group name or photo
• Decide who can send messages (useful for announcement-only groups)

🔕 Muting a Group:
If a group is too busy, tap the group name at the top > 'Mute Notifications' — you still receive messages but your phone won't ring for each one.

🎉 Popular Family Uses:
• Sharing holiday photos
• Organising family events and Christmas plans
• Sharing news and milestones
• Coordinating care for a family member
tips: Mute busy groups so your phone isn't constantly notifying you — you can still read messages at your leisure.
Group chats can have up to 1,024 members — more than enough for even the largest family!
You can leave a group at any time: tap the group name > Scroll to bottom > 'Exit Group'.

lessons.title: Staying Safe on WhatsApp
lessons.content:
WhatsApp is very secure by design, but scams and unwanted contacts are still a risk.

🚨 Common WhatsApp Scams:

1. The 'Hello Mum/Dad' Scam:
You receive a message: 'Hi Mum, it's me, I've got a new phone — can you send me some money?
This is a scam. Always call your family member on their known number to verify before sending any money.

2. Unknown Number Scams:
Strangers message offering jobs, prizes, or investments. Ignore and block.

3. Forwarded Misinformation:
False news, health claims, or chain messages. Do not forward without verifying.

4. Gift Card Scams:
Someone claiming to be a friend or authority figure asks you to buy gift cards. Always a scam.

✅ Privacy Settings:
1. Open WhatsApp > Settings > Privacy
2. Set 'Last Seen' to 'My Contacts' — so strangers cannot see when you were last online
3. Set 'Profile Photo' to 'My Contacts' — so only people you know see your photo
4. Enable 'Advanced — Protect IP address in calls' to prevent callers seeing your approximate location

🔒 Blocking Someone:
• Open the chat > tap their name at the top > Scroll down > 'Block'
• They cannot message you, call you, or see your status

📵 Reporting Spam:
Tap the three dots in any chat > 'Report' — this sends information to WhatsApp without the person knowing.
tips: If you receive 'Hello Mum/Dad', always call your child on their usual number before doing anything else.
Never send money, gift cards, or financial details to anyone via WhatsApp — regardless of who they claim to be.
Adjust your WhatsApp privacy settings so only your contacts can see your profile photo and last seen.

quiz.question: What does it mean when your WhatsApp message shows two BLUE ticks ✓✓?

options.label: The message has been sent from your phone
The message has been delivered to the recipient's phone
The message has been read by the recipient
The recipient has replied

quiz.explanation: One grey tick = sent. Two grey ticks = delivered to their phone. Two BLUE ticks = the recipient has opened and read your message.

quiz.question: You receive a WhatsApp from an unknown number: 'Hi Mum, I have a new phone, please send £300 urgently.' What should you do?

options.label: Send the money — it sounds urgent and is your child
Call your child on their known, saved number to verify before doing anything
Ask them to send a selfie first, then send the money
Send half the money to be safe

quiz.explanation: This is the well-known 'Hello Mum/Dad' scam. Always verify by calling your child on their usual phone number — never send money based on a WhatsApp message from an unknown number.

quiz.question: How do you send a voice note on WhatsApp instead of typing?

options.label: Type 'voice:' before your message
Hold the microphone icon, speak, then release
Tap the camera icon and hold it up to your mouth
Call them instead — you cannot record voice notes

quiz.explanation: Hold the microphone 🎤 icon in the bottom right of any chat window. Speak your message, then release your finger to send it automatically. Slide left to cancel.

quiz.question: A WhatsApp group is sending too many messages and your phone keeps notifying you. What is the best solution?

options.label: Leave the group permanently
Mute the group — you can still read messages without constant notifications
Delete WhatsApp from your phone
Block all group members

quiz.explanation: Muting a group stops notification sounds while still delivering all messages — you can read them at your convenience. Tap the group name > Mute Notifications. You can choose to mute for a set time or forever.

quiz.question: Why is WhatsApp generally better than normal SMS texts for chatting with family abroad?

options.label: WhatsApp messages are longer than texts
WhatsApp uses the internet so international messages and calls are free
WhatsApp is encrypted and texts are not
WhatsApp has better emoji

quiz.explanation: WhatsApp messages and calls use the internet (free on Wi-Fi) rather than the phone network. International SMS and phone calls can be very expensive. With WhatsApp, distance makes no difference to the cost.

quiz.question: How do you prevent strangers from seeing your WhatsApp profile photo?

options.label: Remove your profile photo entirely
In Settings > Privacy, set Profile Photo to 'My Contacts'
Block everyone who is not family
You cannot control who sees your profile photo

quiz.explanation: Go to WhatsApp Settings > Privacy > Profile Photo > and change it to 'My Contacts'. This means only people you have saved in your contacts can see your photo — protecting your privacy from strangers.
